sections.summary: Applies to quick-frozen raw squid and parts offered for direct consumption and excludes products intended for further processing or other industrial purposes.

Defines quick-frozen squid and parts, requires rapid freezing that passes the maximum crystallization range and achieves a thermal-centre temperature of −18°C or colder with maintenance of deep frozen condition, permits controlled industrial repacking with reapplication of quick-freezing, requires processing and packaging to minimize dehydration and oxidation, and allows any presentation provided it meets the standard and is adequately labelled.
Defines quick-frozen squid and parts as obtained from squid species of the families Loliginidae and Ommastrephidae.

Requires the freezing process to be carried out in appropriate equipment to pass the maximum crystallization range quickly and to reach −18°C or colder at the thermal centre after stabilization, mandates maintenance of deep-frozen condition during transport/storage, permits controlled industrial repacking with reapplication of quick-freezing, and requires processing/packaging to minimize dehydration and oxidation.
Permits any product presentation provided it meets all requirements of the standard and is adequately described on the label to avoid misleading or confusing the consumer.

sections.section: STANDARD FOR QUICK FROZEN RAW SQUID
sections.summary: Document title and code: 'STANDARD FOR QUICK FROZEN RAW SQUID, CODEX STAN 191 - 1995' (non‑substantive heading).

Requires quick-frozen squid be prepared from sound squid fit for sale as fresh, specifies glazing water (if used) must be potable or clean sea-water meeting WHO microbiological standards and free from objectionable substances, and states final product compliance is determined by lot examination using specified methods.
Requires quick-frozen squid to be prepared from sound squid of a quality fit to be sold fresh for human consumption.

If glazing is used, the water or glazing solutions must be potable or clean sea-water that meets the microbiological standards of potable water (per WHO drinking-water guidelines) and be free from objectionable substances.

States that products meet the standard when lots examined in accordance with Section 9 comply with the provisions set out in Section 8 and that products shall be examined by the methods given in Section 7.
Prohibits the use of any food additives in these quick-frozen raw squid products.

Recommends preparation and handling per relevant Codex Codes of Practice, requires compliance with any established microbiological criteria, mandates the final product be free from foreign material that poses a health threat, and requires that when tested by prescribed sampling and examination methods the product be free from microorganisms or microbial-origin substances and from any other substances in amounts that may present a hazard to health in accordance with Codex standards.

Sets specific labelling requirements for quick-frozen squid including the product name, presentation reference, use of 'frozen' or 'quick frozen', a statement to maintain quality during transport/storage, glazing declaration if applicable, net contents exclusive of glaze for glazed products, storage temperature instruction of −18°C or colder, and rules for required information on non‑retail containers (name, lot identification, manufacturer/packer and storage instructions must appear on the container; other information may be on accompanying documents or replaced by identifiable marks).

Requires the product name 'squid' (or an authorized alternative), a nearby presentation reference to avoid consumer confusion, labelling of 'frozen' or 'quick frozen' as appropriate, a statement that the product should be maintained to preserve quality during transport/storage, and a declaration if the product has been glazed with sea-water.
Requires that declared net contents for glazed products be exclusive of the glaze.
Label must state that the product shall be stored at a temperature of -18 °C or colder.

Information required for retail labelling may be provided on the non-retail container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the food, lot identification, the name and address of the manufacturer/packer and storage instructions must always appear on the container; lot identification and name/address may be replaced by an identification mark if that mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents.

Section 7 sets sampling and analysis requirements: lot sampling per an appropriate plan with AQL 6.5 and defined sample units, sensory and physical examinations by trained persons per Sections 7.3–7.5/Annex A and CAC/GL 31, net weight determination (including glazed product procedures to be elaborated), thawing procedure (bag immersion ≤35 °C) and defined cooking methods (target internal temperature 65–70 °C) for confirmatory testing.

Lot sampling shall follow an appropriate sampling plan with an AQL of 6.5 (with block sampling per quick-frozen fish block plan); the sample unit is the primary container or at least a 1 kg portion for individually quick-frozen products, and net weight sampling follows CAC criteria.

sections.section: 7.1.2 Sampling of lots for examination of net weight
sections.summary: Sampling of lots for examination of net weight must be carried out according to an appropriate sampling plan meeting the criteria established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission (CAC).

Samples for sensory and physical examination must be assessed by trained persons in accordance with procedures in Sections 7.3–7.5, Annex A, and the Guidelines for the Sensory Evaluation of Fish and Shellfish in Laboratories (CAC/GL 31-1999).
The net weight (exclusive of packaging) of each sample unit representing a lot shall be determined in the frozen state; procedures for products covered by glaze are noted but to be elaborated.

sections.section: 7.3.1 Determination of Net Weight of Product not Covered by Glaze
sections.summary: For products not covered by glaze, the net weight (exclusive of packaging) of each sample unit representing a lot shall be determined in the frozen state.

sections.section:
7.3.2

- Determination of Net Weight of Products Covered by Glaze (to be elaborated)
sections.summary: Non‑substantive placeholder: procedures for determination of net weight of products covered by glaze are not yet elaborated.

Thaw sample units by enclosing in a film-type bag and immersing in water at room temperature (not higher than 35 °C), and consider thawing complete when no hard core of ice crystals remains as judged by gentle squeezing without damaging texture.

Cooking procedures for confirmatory sensory testing are based on heating the product to an internal temperature of 65–70 °C with exact times determined by prior experimentation, and include baking (foil on a sheet), steaming (foil on rack over boiling water), boil-in-bag, and microwave methods (using suitable containers and avoiding odour transfer from plastics).
Heading: sample units are considered defective when they exhibit any of the properties defined in the following subsections.

Deep dehydration is defined as greater than 10% of the surface area showing excessive moisture loss (white or yellow abnormalities) that mask flesh colour, penetrate below the surface, and cannot be removed by scraping without unduly affecting appearance.

Foreign matter is any material in the sample unit not derived from squid (excluding packing), readily recognizable without magnification or detectable by methods including magnification, which does not pose a health threat but indicates non‑compliance with good manufacturing and sanitation practices.

A sample unit is defective for odour and flavour when it exhibits persistent, distinct objectionable odours or flavours indicative of decomposition, which may also be accompanied by light pinkish to red colouration.
Texture is defective when the flesh shows textural breakdown indicative of decomposition, characterized by muscle structure that is mushy or paste-like.

A lot meets the standard when (i) the total number of defectives (per Section 8) does not exceed the acceptance number of an appropriate sampling plan with AQL 6.5, (ii) the average net weight of sample units is not less than declared weight with no unreasonable shortage in any container, and (iii) the Food Additives, Hygiene and Labelling requirements of Sections 4, 5 and 6 are met.

Annex A details sensory and physical examination procedures: complete net weight determination (de-glazing as required), measurement of deep dehydration with a percentage formula, thawing and individual examination for foreign matter and colour, examination using Section 8 criteria (including flesh odour cuts), and confirmatory cooking per Section 7.5 if odour/texture cannot be decided in the thawed state.

Sensory and physical examination steps include net weight determination (per Section 7.3), measuring deep dehydration as percent affected area, thawing and inspecting each squid for foreign matter and colour, evaluating odour and texture per Section 8 (by making a flesh cut), and using defined cooking methods from Section 7.5 to confirm odour, flavour and texture when necessary.
